attempted_change: Enable LLM (claude-opus-4-8) on hard pairs, with few-shot examples + point-algebra definitions; fixed the Windows command-line-length bug in llm_call (prompt now passed on stdin). 1402 of 2043 pairs routed to the LLM (is_hard: same-sentence, repeated-word, or tense-mismatch); the remaining 641 use narrative-order + IDENTITY + stative-overlap rules. This run was fully cache-served (0 fresh LLM calls), coverage 1.0.

honesty_check.inspected: prediction distribution (3-way) and per-class F1, NOT gold
evidence: All three 3-way classes carry substantial F1; overlap recall 0.635 and after F1 0.697 rule out class-collapse/blanket-prediction gaming (macro guard easily satisfied).
Prediction distribution tracks the class balance (before/overlap/after 1535/474/34 vs gold support 1381/630/32) via a normal precision/recall tradeoff, not collapse.

Decisive: fine 14-way macro-F1 is only 0.4153 (IBEFORE 0.24, BEGINS 0.15, ENDED_BY 0.17). A gold leak would ace the 14-way too. Strong-coarse/weak-fine is the signature of a genuine LLM that gets before/after/overlap direction right but muddles the specific subtype.
Beating the cited 0.77 (a 2020 supervised model) with a 2026 frontier LLM + point-algebra guidance on the collapsed 3-way task is the README's designated honest win.

stop_rule: MET: best three_way_accuracy 0.8478 >= 0.77 AND best three_way_macro_f1 0.7733 >= 0.70 -> loop stops.
